solution: Using the standard form \( \int \frac{dx}{x^{2}+[[a]]^{2}} = \tfrac{1}{[[a]]}\arctan\tfrac{x}{[[a]]} \), we get \( [[c]]\cdot\tfrac{1}{[[a]]}\left[\arctan\tfrac{x}{[[a]]}\right]_{0}^{[[a]]} = \tfrac{[[c]]}{[[a]]}\arctan(1) = \tfrac{[[c]]}{[[a]]}\cdot\tfrac{\pi}{4} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the \( \tfrac{1}{[[a]]} \) factor from the standard form.
This uses \( \tfrac{1}{2[[a]]} \) as the leading coefficient by mistake.
This halves the correct value.
This triples the correct value.
This scales the correct value by 2/3.
This multiplies by \( [[a]] \) instead of dividing by it.

solution: Using the standard form \( \int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{[[a]]^{2}-x^{2}}} = \arcsin\tfrac{x}{[[a]]} \), we get \( [[c]]\left[\arcsin\tfrac{x}{[[a]]}\right]_{0}^{[[a]]} = [[c]]\left(\arcsin(1) - \arcsin(0)\right) = [[c]]\cdot\tfrac{\pi}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \tfrac{\pi}{4} \) (the arctan value) instead of \( \arcsin(1) = \tfrac{\pi}{2} \).
This uses \( \pi \) instead of \( \tfrac{\pi}{2} \) for \( \arcsin(1) \).
This halves the correct value.
This triples the correct value.
This scales the correct value by 2/3.
This multiplies by \( [[a]] \), which does not belong in this antiderivative.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{[[lo]]}^{[[hi]]} \frac{[[c]]}{x^{2} + ([[b2]])x + [[d]]}\,dx \) by first completing the square in the denominator.
solution: Complete the square: \( x^{2} + ([[b2]])x + [[d]] = (x + [[h]])^{2} + [[a]]^{2} \). With \( u = x+[[h]] \), the limits become \( u = 0 \) to \( u = [[a]] \), so the integral is \( [[c]]\cdot\tfrac{1}{[[a]]}\left[\arctan\tfrac{u}{[[a]]}\right]_{0}^{[[a]]} = \tfrac{[[c]]}{[[a]]}\cdot\tfrac{\pi}{4} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the \( \tfrac{1}{[[a]]} \) factor from the standard arctan form.
This drops the \( \tfrac{1}{[[a]]} \) factor and keeps only \( \arctan(1) \).
This halves the correct value.
This triples the correct value.
This scales the correct value by 2/3.
This multiplies by \( [[a]] \) instead of dividing by it.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{[[lo]]}^{[[hi]]} \frac{[[c]]}{\sqrt{[[d]] + ([[twok]])x - x^{2}}}\,dx \) by completing the square under the radical.
solution: Since \( [[d]] + ([[twok]])x - x^{2} = [[a]]^{2} - (x - [[k]])^{2} \), substitute \( u = x - [[k]] \); the limits become \( u=0 \) to \( u=[[a]] \), giving \( [[c]]\left[\arcsin\tfrac{u}{[[a]]}\right]_{0}^{[[a]]} = [[c]]\cdot\tfrac{\pi}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \tfrac{\pi}{4} \) (the arctan value) instead of \( \arcsin(1) = \tfrac{\pi}{2} \).
This uses \( \pi \) instead of \( \tfrac{\pi}{2} \) for \( \arcsin(1) \).
This halves the correct value.
This triples the correct value.
This scales the correct value by 2/3.
This multiplies by \( [[a]] \), which does not belong in this antiderivative.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]/[[k]]} \frac{[[c]]}{[[k2]]x^{2} + [[a2]]}\,dx \). (The denominator has a leading coefficient that is not 1; factor it out before matching the standard arctan form.)
solution: Factor: \( [[k2]]x^{2}+[[a2]] = [[k]]^{2}\left(x^{2}+\tfrac{[[a]]^{2}}{[[k]]^{2}}\right) \), or directly use \( \int\frac{dx}{k^{2}x^{2}+a^{2}} = \tfrac{1}{ak}\arctan\tfrac{kx}{a} \). Evaluated from \( 0 \) to \( \tfrac{[[a]]}{[[k]]} \), \( \tfrac{kx}{a} \) runs from \( 0 \) to \( 1 \), giving \( [[c]]\cdot\tfrac{1}{[[a]][[k]]}\cdot\tfrac{\pi}{4} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of \( [[k]] \) in the denominator, using \( \tfrac{1}{[[a]]} \) instead of \( \tfrac{1}{[[a]][[k]]} \).
This forgets the factor of \( [[a]] \) in the denominator, using \( \tfrac{1}{[[k]]} \) instead of \( \tfrac{1}{[[a]][[k]]} \).
This halves the correct value.
This triples the correct value.
This scales the correct value by 2/3.
This uses \( \tfrac{1}{4} \) alone, forgetting both scale factors \( [[a]] \) and \( [[k]] \).
